title: utime on symlink: permission denied in rootless unprivileged container (symlinks become owned by root?)
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: **kind bug**


**Description**

I am not able to extract https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlbuild/archive/0.12.0.tar.gz, it fails in the utime call on a symlink of a directory.
```
tar: ocamlbuild-0.12.0/examples/07-dependent-projects/libdemo: Cannot utime: Operation not permitted
```
As a workaround I can run my container with `--privileged`, but that doesn't work if I want to build a container, because `podman build` doesn't have a `--privileged` flag.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. 
```
cat >Dockerfile.bugmin <<EOF
FROM busybox:latest
RUN adduser -D testuser
USER testuser
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN touch -d @1510420249 -h foo
RUN ln -s foo foo-symlink
RUN touch -d @1510420249 -h foo-symlink
EOF
podman build -t bug -f Dockerfile.bugmin

cat >Dockerfile <<EOF
FROM ubuntu:latest
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y wget strace && apt-get clean
RUN useradd testuser
USER testuser
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN wget https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlbuild/archive/0.12.0.tar.gz
EOF
podman build -t bug-orig -f Dockerfile
podman run -it bug-orig
tar xf 0.12.0.tar.gz
^D
podman run --privileged -it bug-orig
tar xf 0.12.0.tar.gz
^D
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
STEP 12: RUN touch -d @1510420249 -h foo-symlink
touch: foo-symlink: Operation not permitted
error building at step {Env:[PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[touch -d @1510420249 -h foo-symlink] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN touch -d @1510420249 -h foo-symlink Original:RUN touch -d @1510420249 -h foo-symlink}: error while running runtime: exit status 1
$ podman run --privileged -it bug-orig
testuser@e1a58f040af0:/tmp$ tar xf 0.12.0.tar.gz
tar: ocamlbuild-0.12.0/examples/07-dependent-projects/libdemo: Cannot utime: Operation not permitted
tar: Exiting with failure status due to previous errors
$ podman run --privileged -it bug-orig
testuser@e6b4265e2b28:/tmp$ tar xf 0.12.0.tar.gz
testuser@e6b4265e2b28:/tmp$ exit
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Able to extract my tarball, set modification/access times on symlink.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
If I run podman rootless, but I am root inside my container then updating the timestamp works.
If I change to a regular user inside the container it fails as can be seen above.
If I run the same container as privileged (but still rootless) then everything works.

This was initially discovered while tracking down another bug: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2053, but is otherwise unrelated to that bug.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Version:       0.12.1.2
Go Version:    go1.11.2
Git Commit:    "67ab7549b44484cc3f201d7bb2b58b922f8edc24"
Built:         Thu Dec 13 19:35:26 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

  BuildahVersion: 1.6-dev
  Conmon:

  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: bolt
  kernel: 4.19.13-300.fc29.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 2h 45m 48.5s (Approximately 0.08 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []

  - docker.io

  ContainerStore:
    number: 44
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /var/home/edwin/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 34
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fedora 29 Silverblue

comments.body: Can you check if this works with root running podman?
Yes, if I use `sudo` to run the above `podman build` and `podman run` commands they all work.
Most likely it is something blocked by usernamespace then.
It sounds like it's allowed by privileged rootless containers - so probably not user namespaces directly, but something we drop when privileged is set.

Works for me.

```
$ cat >Dockerfile.bugmin <<EOF

> RUN adduser -D testuser
> USER testuser
> WORKDIR /tmp
> RUN touch -d @1510420249 -h foo
> RUN ln -s foo foo-symlink
> RUN touch -d @1510420249 -h foo-symlink
> EOF
$ podman build -t bug -f /tmp/Dockerfile.bugmin /tmp

@rhatdan Are you missing an untar of 0.12.0.tar.gz in your reproducer?

`--privileged` (or running with `sudo`) works, however the 2 `--security-opt` flags do not:
```
$ podman run --security-opt seccomp=unconfined --security-opt label=disable -it bug-orig
testuser@53c41ea83cfd:/tmp$ tar xf 0.12.0.tar.gz 
tar: ocamlbuild-0.12.0/examples/07-dependent-projects/libdemo: Cannot utime: Operation not permitted

$ podman run --privileged -it bug-orig
testuser@2f9eb8255c29:/tmp$ tar xf 0.12.0.tar.gz 
testuser@2f9eb8255c29:/tmp$ exit
```

FWIW if I stay `root` inside the container, but just unprivileged outside that works too:
```
$ cat >Dockerfile.root <<EOF
FROM ubuntu:latest
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y wget strace && apt-get clean
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN wget https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlbuild/archive/0.12.0.tar.gz
EOF
$ podman build -t bug-root -f Dockerfile.root
$ podman run -it bug-root
root@51f67397a94c:/tmp# tar xf 0.12.0.tar.gz 
root@51f67397a94c:/tmp# exit
```

`root` inside a rootless container is actually your user on the host, via a userns mapping - so it's not security-essential to do things as a non-root user with rootless containers.

@giuseppe Do we drop any additional privileges/capabilities as we switch to a different user in a rootless container?

It looks like `CAP_FOWNER` makes it work:
```
podman run --cap-add FOWNER -it bug-orig
testuser@387246b744d9:/tmp$ tar xf 0.12.0.tar.gz 
testuser@387246b744d9:/tmp$ exit
```

It looks like the symbolic link becomes owned by root, and therefore without CAP_FOWNER we have no permission to change it, so the real bug here seems to be: why are the symlinks, created by `testuser` inside the container, owned by root?

```
podman run -it bug-orig
testuser@e8f04ff2841f:/tmp$ touch foo
testuser@e8f04ff2841f:/tmp$ ls -l foo
-rw-rw-rw-. 1 testuser testuser 0 Jan  7 00:18 foo
testuser@e8f04ff2841f:/tmp$ ln -s foo foo-link
testuser@e8f04ff2841f:/tmp$ ls -l foo-link 
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root 3 Jan  7 00:18 foo-link -> foo
testuser@e8f04ff2841f:/tmp$ stat foo-link
  File: foo-link -> foo
  Size: 3         	Blocks: 0          IO Block: 4096   symbolic link
Device: 54h/84d	Inode: 14423889    Links: 1
Access: (0777/lrwxrwxrwx)  Uid: (    0/    root)   Gid: (    0/    root)
Access: 2019-01-07 00:18:59.447721392 +0000
Modify: 2019-01-07 00:18:53.823773471 +0000
Change: 2019-01-07 00:18:53.823773471 +0000
 Birth: -
```

Run this with `sudo` and the permissions on the link are fine:
```
sudo podman run -it bug
testuser@541ca2c08d22:/tmp$ tar xf 0.12.0.tar.gz 
testuser@541ca2c08d22:/tmp$ touch foo
testuser@541ca2c08d22:/tmp$ ln -s foo foo-link
testuser@541ca2c08d22:/tmp$ stat foo-link
  File: foo-link -> foo
  Size: 3         	Blocks: 0          IO Block: 4096   symbolic link
Device: 54h/84d	Inode: 1360462     Links: 1
Access: (0777/lrwxrwxrwx)  Uid: ( 1000/testuser)   Gid: ( 1000/testuser)
Access: 2019-01-07 00:20:18.885985728 +0000
Modify: 2019-01-07 00:20:18.885985728 +0000
Change: 2019-01-07 00:20:18.885985728 +0000
 Birth: -
```
```
It is an issue in fuse-overlayfs: https://github.com/containers/fuse-overlayfs/pull/37
